Admission Date:  1985-11-28              Discharge Date:   1993-01-31

Date of Birth:  2004-09-30             Sex:   M

Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:
Niacin

Attending:Bradley
Chief Complaint:
(STEMI)

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
cypher stent placement
cardiac catheterization


History of Present Illness:
59 y/o man with hx. LAD stent (? date) who apparently underwent
surgery at approx 1300 today at the VA for 'deviated septum'. He
had been off of his aspirin for the preceding 5 days (presumably
in anticipation of surgery). Per report, either after or during
surgery he was given some intranasal coacaine (for bleeding). In
post-op after the surgery, he got up to go to the bathroom, and
complained of acute onset of chest pressure (1.5 hours after
surgery). He appeared to be having acute, anterior, STEMI on ECG
so was sent to Rehabilitation Institute Of Chicago for emergent management. In the ED, his ECG
was consistent with anterior stemi, he was plavix loaded,
started on heparin gtt with 5000 U bolus, started on integrelin
with bolus, given aspirin, nitroglycerin gtt, and taken to the
cath. lab. There he was found to have LMCA patent, LAD with
proximal occlusion of previous stent, T.O of OM1 with distal
collateral perfusion, RCA dominant with moderate, diffuse,
disease and supplying collateral perfusion to the OM. His wedge
pressure mean was 24. He was given 20 of IV lasix times one. He
undewent cypher stenting of the in-stent thrombosis of his prior
LAD stent. About half-way through the procedure his nose started
bleeding. Integrellin was d/c'd; ENT was called, the
administered bilateral NC suction and "merocel" packing
bilaterally with control of bleeding. He is transfered to the
CCU for further management.

Past Medical History:
HTN, HCL, MI s/p LAD cypher stent 2016

Social History:
rare etoh, last in [**December 2186**]. Ongoing smoking for past 45 y, 1
ppd.

Family History:
No family history of premature coronary artery disease or sudden
death.

Physical Exam:
Blood pressure was 137/80 mm Hg while supine. Pulse was 84
beats/min and regular, respiratory rate was 14 breaths/min.
Generally the patient was well developed, well nourished and
well groomed. The patient was oriented to person, place and
time. The patient's mood and affect were not inappropriate.
.
There was no xanthalesma and conjunctiva were pink with no
pallor or cyanosis of the oral mucosa. The neck was supple with
JVP of 7 cm. The carotid waveform was normal. There was no
thyromegaly. The were no chest wall deformities, scoliosis or
kyphosis. The respirations were not labored and there were no
use of accessory muscles. The lungs were clear to ascultation
bilaterally with normal breath sounds and no adventitial sounds
or rubs.
.
Palpation of the heart revealed the PMI to be located in the 5th
intercostal space, mid clavicular line. There were no thrills,
lifts or palpable S3 or S4. The heart sounds revealed a normal
S1 and the S2 was normal. There were no rubs, murmurs, clicks or
gallops.
.
The abdominal aorta was not enlarged by palpation. There was no
hepatosplenomegaly or tenderness. The abdomen was soft nontender
and nondistended. The extremities had no pallor, cyanosis,
clubbing or edema. There were no abdominal, femoral or carotid
bruits. Inspection and/or palpation of skin and subcutaneous
tissue showed no stasis dermatitis, ulcers, scars, or xanthomas.

Cardiac catheterization on 1985-11-28:

BRIEF HISTORY:   59 year old man with known CAD s/p stent
placement to
the pLAD in 05-21 stopped ASA for rhinoplasty for deviated septum
where
some intranasal cocain was applied for hemostasis. Shortly after
the
procedure, he developed chest pain.  EKG shows anterior ST
elevations
with reciprocal ST depression in I and slight ST elevation in
III.  He
was sent to the North Shore Medical Center cardiac catheterization laboratory for
anterior
STEMI.

INDICATIONS FOR CATHETERIZATION:
Coronary artery disease, Canadian Heart Class IV,  unstable.

PROCEDURE:
Right Heart Catheterization: was performed by percutaneous entry
of the
right femoral vein, using a 7 French pulmonary wedge pressure
catheter,
advanced to the PCW position through an 8 French introducing
sheath.
Cardiac output was measured by the Fick method.
Left Heart Catheterization: was performed by percutaneous entry
of the
right femoral artery, using a 5 French right Perez catheter,
advanced
to the ascending aorta through a 6 French introducing sheath.
Coronary Angiography: was performed in multiple projections
using a 6
French XB LAD guide and a 5 French JR4 catheter, with manual
contrast
injections.
Percutaneous coronary revascularization was performed using
placement of
drug-eluting stent(s).
Conscious Sedation:  was provided with appropriate monitoring
performed by
a member of the nursing staff.

HEMODYNAMICS RESULTS BODY SURFACE AREA:  2.26 m2
  HEMOGLOBIN: 16.5 gms %
  FICK
**PRESSURES
RIGHT ATRIUM {a/v/m} 17/17/13
RIGHT VENTRICLE {s/ed} 49/13
PULMONARY WEDGE {a/v/m} 30/28/24
AORTA {s/d/m} 116/81/76
**CARDIAC OUTPUT

RHYTHM SINUS
O2 CONS. IND {ml/min/m2} 125
A-V O2 DIFFERENCE {ml/ltr} 65
CARD. OP/IND FICK {l/mn/m2} 4.4/1.9
**RESISTANCES
SYSTEMIC VASC. RESISTANCE 1146

**% SATURATION DATA (NL)
PA MAIN 65
AO 92



**ARTERIOGRAPHY RESULTS MORPHOLOGY % STENOSIS COLLAT. FROM
**RIGHT CORONARY
1) PROXIMAL RCA   DIFFUSELY DISEASED
2) MID RCA   DIFFUSELY DISEASED
2A) ACUTE MARGINAL   NORMAL
3) DISTAL RCA   NORMAL
4) R-PDA   NORMAL
4A) R-POST-LAT   NORMAL

**ARTERIOGRAPHY RESULTS MORPHOLOGY % STENOSIS COLLAT. FROM
**LEFT CORONARY
5) LEFT MAIN   NORMAL
6) PROXIMAL LAD   DISCRETE 100
12) PROXIMAL CX   NORMAL
13) MID CX   NORMAL
13A) DISTAL CX   NORMAL
14) OBTUSE MARGINAL-1   DISCRETE 100
15) OBTUSE MARGINAL-2   NORMAL


**PTCA RESULTS
  LAD
**BASELINE
STENOSIS PRE-PTCA 100
**TECHNIQUE
GUIDING CATH 6F XB [**Name Prefix (Prefixes) **]
[**Last Name (Prefixes) 34084**] PT GRAPH
INITIAL BALLOON (mm) 2
FINAL BALLOON (mm) 3.5
# INFLATIONS 4
MAX PRESSURE (PSI) 300
**RESULT
STENOSIS POST-PTCA 0
SUCCESS? (Y/N) Y

PTCA COMMENTS:     The angiogram showed occlusive stent
thrombosis in
the mid LAD. The strategy was to stent this lesion. We used
Heparin,
Integrilin, Aspirin and Clopidogrel prophylactically. A 6F XB
LAD 3.5
guide provided adequate support. A PT Robert Olson Intermediate wire
crossed
in to the distal vessel easily. We then performed thrombectomy
with a
quick cat catheter with some resolution of thrombus. We then
predilated
the lesion with a 2.0 X 15 mm Voyager balloon up to 10 atm. We
then
deployed a 3.0 X 28mm Cypher stent in to the lesion at14 atm.
The stent
was post dilated with a 3.5 X 20 mm balloon up to 20 atm. The
final
angiogram showed TIMI III flow with no residual stenosis, no
dissection,
no perforation and no embolisation. The patient left the lab in
a stable
condition.


TECHNICAL FACTORS:
Total time (Lidocaine to test complete) = 0 hour47 minutes.
Arterial time = 0 hour44 minutes.
Fluoro time = 8 minutes.
Contrast injected:
          Non-ionic low osmolar (isovue, optiray...), vol 90 ml,
Indications - Renal
Anesthesia:
          1% Lidocaine subq.
Anticoagulation:
          Heparin 1977 units IV
Other medication:
          Fentanyl 75mcg IV
          Integrillin (75mg/100ml) 16ml/hr IV
          Integrillin (2mg/ml) bolus 9ml IV
          Midazolam 0.5mg IV
Cardiac Cath Supplies Used:
          .014 Gina Ramirez, PT Gina Ramirez, 300CM
          2.0 GUIDANT, VOYAGER 15
          3.5 [**Company **], QUANTUM MAVERICK, 20
          6F CORDIS, XBLAD 3.5
          3.0 CORDIS, CYPHER OTW, 28
          - Brandt-NASH, QUICKCAT
          - ALLEGIANCE, CUSTOM STERILE PACK
          - GUIDANT, PRIORITY PACK 20/30


COMMENTS:
1. Selective coronary angiography of this right dominant system
demonstrates severe 2 vessel cornary artery disease.  The LMCA
is
without angiographically apparent disease.  There is stent
thrombosis
of the pLAD which was totally occluded the site of the
previously placed
stent. The LCx gives off OM1 which is chronically totally
occluded and
fills via robust right to left collaterals.  The RCA has
moderate
diffuse disease that is non-obstructive and supplies collaterals
to the
LCx system.
2.  Resting hemodynamic measurements demonstrates moderately
elevated
biventricular filling pressure (LVEDP of 24mmHg and RVEDP of
13mmHg,
respectively).  There is moderate pulmonary hypertension with
the RV
systolic pressure of 49mmHg.  The cardiac index is depressed at
1.9L/m2.
3. Left ventriculography was deferred since the patient will
have a
non-invasive assessment of his LV function .
4. The lesion in the instent thrombosis in the mid LAD was
predilated
with a 2.5 mm balloon, stented with a 3.0 mm Cypher stent and
post
dilated with a 3.5 mm balloon with lesion reduction to 0%. The
final
angiogram showed TIMI III flow with no residual stenosis, no
dissection
and no embolisation. (see PTCA comments)

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
1. Acute MI
2. Severe 2 vessel coronary artery disease
3. Elevated biventricular filling pressure
4. Successful stenting of the LAD (Drug eluting)
5. Moderate pulmonary hypertension
6. Depressed cardiac output.

MEASUREMENTS:
Left Atrium - Long Axis Dimension: *4.1 cm (nl <= 4.0 cm)
Left Atrium - Four Chamber Length: 4.5 cm (nl <= 5.2 cm)
Right Atrium - Four Chamber Length: 4.5 cm (nl <= 5.0 cm)
Left Ventricle - Septal Wall Thickness: *1.2 cm (nl 0.6 - 1.1
cm)
Left Ventricle - Inferolateral Thickness: *1.2 cm (nl 0.6 - 1.1
cm)
Left Ventricle - Diastolic Dimension: 5.3 cm (nl <= 5.6 cm)
Left Ventricle - Ejection Fraction: 20% to 30% (nl >=55%)
Aorta - Valve Level: *4.0 cm (nl <= 3.6 cm)
Aorta - Ascending: 3.4 cm (nl <= 3.4 cm)
Aortic Valve - Peak Velocity: 1.0 m/sec (nl <= 2.0 m/sec)
Mitral Valve - E Wave: 0.5 m/sec
Mitral Valve - A Wave: 0.8 m/sec
Mitral Valve - E/A Ratio: 0.63
Mitral Valve - E Wave Deceleration Time: 234 msec
TR Gradient (+ RA = PASP): 16 to 20 mm Hg (nl <= 25 mm Hg)

INTERPRETATION:

Findings:

LEFT ATRIUM: Mild LA enlargement.

RIGHT ATRIUM/INTERATRIAL SEPTUM: Normal RA size. No ASD by 2D or
color
Doppler.

LEFT VENTRICLE: Mild symmetric LVH. Normal LV cavity size. No LV

mass/thrombus. Severely depressed LVEF. No resting LVOT
gradient. No VSD.

RIGHT VENTRICLE: Normal RV chamber size and free wall motion.

AORTA: Moderately dilated aortic sinus. Normal ascending aorta
diameter.

AORTIC VALVE: Normal aortic valve leaflets (3). No AS. No AR.

MITRAL VALVE: Normal mitral valve leaflets. No MVP. Normal
mitral valve
supporting structures. Mild (1+) MR. Normal LV inflow pattern
for age.

TRICUSPID VALVE: Normal tricuspid valve leaflets with trivial
TR. Normal
tricuspid valve supporting structures. Normal PA systolic
pressure.

PULMONIC VALVE/PULMONARY ARTERY: Normal pulmonic valve leaflets
with
physiologic PR. Normal main PA. No Doppler evidence for PDA

PERICARDIUM: No pericardial effusion.

Conclusions:
The left atrium is mildly dilated. No atrial septal defect is
seen by 2D or  color Doppler. There is mild symmetric left
ventricular hypertrophy. The left  ventricular cavity size is
normal. No masses or thrombi are seen in the left  ventricle.
Overall left ventricular systolic function is severely depressed
 (ejection fraction 20-30 percent) secondary to akinesis of the
anterior  septum, severe hypokinesis of the anterior free wall
and inferior septum, with  extensive apical akinesis and focal
apical dyskinesis. There is no ventricular  septal defect. Right
ventricular chamber size and free wall motion are normal.
The aortic root is moderately dilated athe sinus level. The
aortic valve
leaflets (3) appear structurally normal with good leaflet
excursion and no  aortic regurgitation. The mitral valve
leaflets are structurally normal. There  is no mitral valve
prolapse. Mild (1+) mitral regurgitation is seen. The  estimated
pulmonary artery systolic pressure is normal. There is no
pericardial effusion.

Impression: extensive anteroseptal/apical infarct
  Electronically signed by [**First Name11 (Name Pattern1) 449**] Jackson, MD on 2005-11-11
16:12.

Brief Hospital Course:
ASSESSMENT AND PLAN, AS REVIEWED AND DISCUSSED IN
MULTIDISCIPLINARY ROUNDS: 59 y/o with hx. MI and LAD stent 2016
who underwent nasal septum surgery at JP Rockdale Hospital And Health System Hospital on 1985-11-28
and suffered anterior STEMI 1.5 hours post-op. Transferred to
North Shore Medical Center for further management. Received cyper to LAD for stent
thrombosis. Active issues during this hospital course include:
.
# STEMI: Occurred in the setting of stopping aspirin for 5 days
pre-op, nasal cocaine administration intra-operatively and
anesthesia. He was intially started on integrilin, but then
during cardiac cath developed severe epistaxis. He received a
Cypher stent to LAD.  Patient will NEED TO CONTINUE ON Plavix
FOR ONE YEAR, AND ALSO DAILY aspirin.  He is also on ACE I,
Statin, BB per his outpatient regimen. Smoking cessation was
encouraged and nicotine patch placed.  He will need follow-up in
smokin cessation clinic at VA.  Post-catheterization
echocardiogram revealed depressed EF 20-30%, secondary to
wall-motion abnormalities as above.
.
# EPISTAXIS: Status-post nasal septum repair. Bleeding started
in the setting of integrilin/heparin administration during
cardiac cath for STEMI. Now stopped with nasal packing and
discontinuation of heparin/integrillin. ENT was called and
packed his nose and stopped bleeding. He will need packing for 3
more days from day of discharge. Cefazolin was given for
prophylaxis.
.
# HTN: Continue beta-blocker. Restarted home medications.
.
# DYSLIPIDEMIA: Continue statin and gemfibrozil.

Medications on Admission:
PPI
Femlodipine 5
Gemfibrozil 600 Rehabilitation Institute Of Chicago
Metoprolol XL 200
ASA 325
Simvastatin 80
Lisinopril 40

Discharge Disposition:
Home

Discharge Diagnosis:
Primary:
ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Severe epistaxis
Hypertension
Dyslipidemia


Discharge Condition:
stable, ambulating, pain free


Discharge Instructions:
You had a heart attack and received a stent in your coronary
arteries which had thrombosed. The stent reopened the coronary
artery.
.
Please take all medications as directed, especially the aspirin
and Plavix, every day.  It is very important that you have the
prescription for Plavix filled today, because you just had a
stent placed.
.
Please call 911 or go to the emergency room if you have any
symptoms of chest pain, chest pressure, shortness of breath,
fever, chills, nausea, vomiting, leg swelling or any other
concerning symptoms.

Followup Instructions:
Please go to 8321 Cohen Forks
Gonzalesmouth, NE 01796-7343 urgent care to have your
prescription for Plavix filled TODAY.  Please make sure you have
that prescription filled today.

Please call to make an appointment to follow-up with your
primary care physician.

You have an appointment with the Ear-Nose-Throat specialist on
2010-01-12 at 1:40 PM at the VA.  Please call the VA ENT
office to change your appointment.

You have an appointment with your cardiologist, Dr. Clark
at 431 Joel Greens
Lake Andrewborough, AS 03594-6702 VA on 1973-11-15 at 4:00 PM.  Please call his
office to change the appointment.
